reviews.author: Nadine Hawkins
reviews.text: Be aware. The main watefalls entry isn’t open (as of today 28th February 2026), it looks like it’s not Tet related and will be closed for some time. No reason for the closure given just massive signs up saying it’s closed until new announcement. BUT if you go 1 minute round the corner to a place called Elephant Coffee not only does it have an amazing perch right on top of the waterfalls it also has an entrance way down to the falls. 100% must visit

reviews.author: Wong TF
reviews.text: This Elephant Waterfall will be one of the 3 waterfalls if one go for local tours in Dalat. The surrouding area is well developed compared to the other 2 waterfalls. Apart from visiting this waterfall (which is closed now), there is also a nice pagoda nearby (Linh An Pagoda).

reviews.author: Nancy Trekker
reviews.text: Be advised, as of Feb 1, 2026, the lower access to the waterfall is closed until further notice. You can still get a great view of the falls from Chùa Linh An (the temple).

reviews.author: Marlon
reviews.text: Great Waterfall and nice surroundings. If you like to enter it for free there were some amazing locals which showed us the way to see the waterfall from the bottom. Just pass through the PAGODA on the left hand side and turn left afterwards. Now you can drive down with the scooter /Motorbike till the road ends. After 2 minutes walk you're straight at the front. Enjoy!

reviews.author: Jim Reaugh
reviews.text: Elephant waterfall — where nature’s got the volume cranked up to eleven. This place is the ultimate reminder that Mother Nature can be a show-off when she wants to be. The roar of the waterfall is so powerful, you’ll be half expecting David Attenborough to start narrating your visit... Getting there? Well, let’s just say it’s a bit of an adventure. The path down is steep, a little rugged, and let’s be honest, you might end up with a muddy souvenir or two on your shoes. But trust me, it’s all part of the charm. Just think of it as nature’s way of saying, ‘Hey, you’re earning this view, pal!’ And what a view it is. You get up close and personal with one of the most breathtaking waterfalls in Vietnam. The mist from the falls is practically nature’s own cooling system—perfect for those hot, humid days. And the surrounding lush greenery? It’s like stepping into a real-life jungle book. Oh, and don’t forget to check out the rock formations underneath the falls. They’re like these natural sculptures that make you appreciate just how wild and wonderful this planet really is.

reviews.author: norwahida yaakub
reviews.text: The actual view not here. You must search elephant coffee shop. Because the thác Voi entrance from here ( elephant coffee shop). The entrance fee is 50vnd per person.. stunning view..

reviews.author: Eric Berberich
reviews.text: An ok waterfall - can be visited next to Lady Buddha ..... there is around 100 steps down to see it from lower level - and from the café at the top you can see the edge of the waterfall. The cave behind the waterfall is closed for public access.

reviews.author: Des P
reviews.text: Beautiful area - well worth the visit

reviews.author: Paul Anderson
reviews.text: Inaccessible.. drone or long lens only...

reviews.author: le thang
reviews.text: The main entrance are closed currently, but you still can get in by asking the security and pay to him, this would give you a better view to the falls than going in through the Elephant coffee shop at the other side of the waterfalls

reviews.author: Ренат
reviews.text: It is considered the most powerful waterfall in the province. But for now the entrance to it is closed. You can see it from the nearby Buddhist pagoda.

reviews.author: Kamila K
reviews.text: The waterfall is closed. However, you can get closer from the Elephant Cafe for 50,000 VND There is a lot of trash in the water and around the waterfall.

reviews.author: rai nguyen
reviews.text: Breathtaking view and fresh breeze. The main entrance was closed so you'll have to go to elephant coffee shop to get in

reviews.author: Phuckyu Googlescumbaggs
reviews.text: The actual entrance is temporarily closed, but you can get access via the cafe. Though they will charge you an entry fee. Other fellow reviewers have suggested the fee is 60k. I arrived on my way from DaLat to Bao Loc on a day that was particularly rainy and pressed for time, so I didn't try to get access to the waterfall via the cafe. I only saw the waterfall from the nearby pagoda Chua Linh An. You can easily see the falls from the platform at the base of the large Quan Yin Buddha statue. (NB: The pagoda is free to enter and has a large free parking area.)

reviews.author: Joao Morais
reviews.text: Beautiful place, there’s a pagoda right next to it. For a better and safer view cross the bridge and see it from the opposite side of the google pin.

reviews.author: Alexander Ramsay
reviews.text: Beautiful place. Wonderful views. Free and self-guided. Please be respectful and remove shoes when inside as this is a temple.

reviews.author: Рада Зверева
reviews.text: Attention!!! It is closed now. The woman from the shop send me to the cafe on another side. There you can pay 30.000 and see whaterfall. I think that view from cafe is not so impressive

reviews.author: Edgar Kozubovskij
reviews.text: This is a Huuuge one, if I would be you my dear reader I would add this waterfall to the "Want to go" list 😁

reviews.author: Ziv Amir
reviews.text: We paid to enter the site with the waterfall and it was 1 minute and that's it. Wasn't what we expected, so the lady told us to go to see the waterfall on other side next to the big Buddha lady, so we basically had a day of some walking- not bad, but not what we thought. Don't recommend.

reviews.author: Travel Here Youtube
reviews.text: In June 2024 this place was closed. The road / gate next to the pagoda was also closed

reviews.author: Ryan Savidan
reviews.text: Yep it's a closed but not really closed situation type deal. 50k to let you in, the steps down are in good shape considering it's been closed for 3 years now. Just watch your step, use rhe hand rails and take it slow. There is no trail to go super close to the waterfall, don't try to go near. The stairs up if off to the side, by the grassy area to the right at the end of the bottom of the trail.

reviews.author: Chloé de Malézieux
reviews.text: Do not let your tour guide take you there if not for free! Our made us pay 50k each for an obscure woman pretendingly building the road (50m..) but I honestly think she might be building herself a house on our backs. Plus from down the road the view point is really not that good :/ We felt a little betrayed by our guide but well that's the game when you are a naive tourist..

reviews.author: Violaine Martin
reviews.text: Only free way to go in has not the best view. A lot of mud in the water

reviews.author: APANDA VIETNAM TRAVEL (THAO BEE.)
reviews.text: Beautiful waterfall besides wonderful pagoda.

reviews.author: Huu quang Huynh
reviews.text: Actually the water fall was suspended from Jan 2023 but the Linh Ung pagoda next to it is open free. From the backyard garden of the pagoda you can have a full good view of the fall and take some nice photos. Besides, there is a giant statue of Budda Goddess with stairs inside for you to climb to the top.

reviews.author: Georgia Mills
reviews.text: Smells, extremely dirty. It says closed but you can pay the guy inside the gate. Just walk to the top of the temple statue next door and look out the window for free and you won’t get the smell.

reviews.author: Arnaud e
reviews.text: Very peaceful place and beautiful waterfall. Temple is unique, and you’ll visit a wonderfully statue too

reviews.author: B J
reviews.text: Super shaddy and dangerous to visit right now lol And it's super dirty. The pagoda near by is worth the visit its very peaceful and tranquil. Instead for an UNREAL and very clean waterfall go to the pongour waterfalls not too far away instead.

reviews.author: Chau Felice
reviews.text: Beautiful waterful! Imagine way to enjoy it!

reviews.author: Martin Dulák
reviews.text: Tip: watch the falls from the nearby Buddhist statue.
